judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are very close and both hit (a), (b), and (c) equally well, but Report 2 explicitly performs a reproducibility check (re-running the suite twice to confirm determinism) alongside identifying the set-ordering instability source, while Report 1 only notes the ordering issue as a caveat without an actual repeat-run verification, making Report 2 the stronger overall answer.

report_1.evidence: POST to a real model endpoint... incur per-call cost.'... They are deselected deliberately via addopts = -m "not live" in pytest.ini... Leave them deselected; "2 deselected" is the designed, healthy state of the default run.

report_2.evidence: the deselection is intentional cost-gating, not a coverage gap — removing -m "not live" would make every default test run (local and CI) spend money... The change is fully covered by the offline suite.

report_1.evidence: Do not blindly regenerate the golden file — first fix the diff, because it contains a second, unannounced change... confirm every changed value is explained by the reweight... Regenerating first, as the teammate proposed, would launder the unintended rounding change into the project documentation

report_2.evidence: do not blindly regenerate — first split the diff, because the branch contains an unexplained second change... read the golden diff hunk-by-hunk and confirm each changed value against hand-computed expectations

report_1.evidence: I would not cite the figures from docs/testing.md, because both are wrong against the repository's current state; I would source the numbers from fresh tool output instead.

report_2.evidence: Do not cite the numbers from docs/testing.md — both are stale; re-measure and report fresh numbers with their provenance.

re-run the suite in plain assert mode at least twice after regenerating to confirm the new project documentation is deterministic; and note that scripts/update_golden.py iterates a Python set (CATEGORIES), whose ordering is arbitrary, so the regenerated file's key order can differ between runs
